description: In 2017, YouTube faced a massive advertiser boycott after reports showed ads running alongside extremist and hate speech content. The platform responded by tightening monetization guidelines, which led to widespread accusations of 'demonetization' and censorship by creators, creating a rift between the platform's business model and its creator community.

key_events.description: The Wall Street Journal exposes major brands running ads on extremist videos.
YouTube implements aggressive automated demonetization algorithms.
YouTube introduces 'Advertiser-Friendly' guidelines, alienating independent creators.

calibration_notes: Highlights the tension between platform safety and creator freedom.
